steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two parts of the passage
steps.content: The passage first describes Franklin embedding blue threads and muscovite into paper as anti-counterfeiting measures. The blank then introduces a completely separate technique: stamping notes with sage leaf imprints. The relationship is additive — a second independent method is being added.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Specifically,': Signals that what follows is a specific detail or elaboration of the preceding idea — but the sage leaf technique is a distinct method, not a specification of the threading method. ✗
  • Choice B — 'That said,': Signals a contrast or qualification — but the passage is adding another technique, not walking back or contrasting with the first. ✗
  • Choice C — 'For example,': Signals that what follows is an instance of a category just mentioned — but the sage leaf technique is not an example of the threading method; it is a separate measure. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Moreover,': Signals an additional point of the same kind — correct for introducing a second anti-counterfeiting technique alongside the first. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
